name: Garlic Mud Chicken Bgnj11a
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 325°F.
In a blender, combine peeled garlic cloves with 1/2 cup olive oil and Cajun spices. Blend until creamy.
Add remaining olive oil as needed to achieve desired consistency.
Combine the oil and garlic mixture in a large bowl.
Slowly add flour, mixing until a very heavy, thick, smooth mud-like consistency is achieved.
Tie chicken wings and legs tightly against the sides of the carcass using string.
Using a spatula, completely coat the chicken with the Garlic Mud as evenly as possible. Fill crevices between wings and legs.
Place the chicken on a rack, breast side up, in a pan and put it in the preheated oven.
Roast whole chicken for 1½ to 2½ hours, or until the coating is well browned.
If using cut-up pieces, coat each piece thoroughly, place on a rack, and roast for a shorter time until the coating is brown.
Serve with crisp garlic pieces. Using a vertical roaster (wire stand) accommodates longer cooking times at lower heat settings.
Best results are achieved by following these steps carefully and ensuring even distribution of the Garlic Mud coating.
